column1: Decision table. Match on days_per_week, then experience, then goal, first matching row wins. experience and goal are pipe separated tokens, 'All' matches everything. Focal points never change the split, they change slot filling and Weekly Volume distribution.

column5: One day only ever gets full body.
Beginners get the pattern practice of three full body days.
Frequency beats session focus for these goals.
Full body three times hits every muscle at least twice a week, the current strength standard. PPL3 stays available as an opt-in preference for users who prefer it.
Three lifting days plus a real conditioning day suits a deficit better than four lifting days.
Upper Lower is the four day answer.
A beginner's fifth day is conditioning, not more lifting.
The fifth day is built from focal points.
Four lifting days plus one conditioning day.
Beginners are not served six training days. Four is the ceiling.
Six real training days, everything twice.
Four lifting days, two conditioning days.
